patch_description: Add temporal segmentation features to capture within-walk gait deterioration and freezing episodes. The current handcrafted features are computed on the entire sequence, which averages out important temporal dynamics like gait freezing or deterioration in moderate-to-severe PD. By splitting each walk into 4 non-overlapping segments and computing per-segment statistics, we can capture the variability of gait patterns within a single walk. This is particularly important for the mild/severe boundary where 58 severe cases are currently misclassified. We compute the standard deviation of per-segment means across all feature categories, adding 144 dimensions of temporal variability that directly proxy the within-walk fluctuations characteristic of moderate-to-severe PD. This change targets the primary bottleneck of severe class F1 by adding discriminative features for gait instability and freezing episodes that are invisible to whole-sequence statistics.
